title: Fixed-PVM Born Rule Uniqueness from Fisher Non-Expansion and Operational Calibration
abstract: Fix a finite dimension $d \geq 2$ and a fixed rank-1 PVM $M=\{|e_1\rangle\langle e_1|,\ldots,|e_d\rangle\langle e_d|\}$ on ${\bf C}^d$. Let $P_M:\mathbb{CP}^{d-1}\toΔ^{d-1}$ be a readout map on pure states. We prove that three primitives force the Born rule for this fixed measurement: (i) square-root regularity of $R_M=\sqrt{P_M}$ along Fubini-Study geodesics, (ii) the universal readout Cramer-Rao bound $F_{\rm cl}\leq F_Q$ on smooth pure-state curves, and (iii) operational calibration on basis preparations $P_M([e_i])=δ_i$. The geometric core is a rigidity theorem for Fisher-non-expanding self-maps of the probability simplex: after conjugation by the square-root chart, such maps become round-metric 1-Lipschitz self-maps of the positive spherical orthant, and vertex fixing forces the identity. The main readout theorem is dimensionwise, fixed-PVM, and pure-state only. Escort-class Born uniqueness and the Markov/coarse-graining routes appear as corollaries or alternative routes.
summary: This paper proves that the Born rule is the unique way to map pure states to measurement probabilities for a fixed basis, provided the map respects certain geometric bounds and correctly identifies basis states. It uses a rigidity theorem for 1-Lipschitz maps to show that the requirement of not expanding Fisher information effectively 'freezes' the map to the standard Born rule.
main_problem: Determining whether the Born rule is the unique readout map for a fixed rank-1 projective measurement (PVM) under specific geometric and operational constraints.
main_result: The paper proves that the conjunction of square-root regularity, the universal readout Cramér-Rao bound, and operational calibration on basis preparations uniquely forces the readout map to be the Born rule.
method: A geometric rigidity approach using the Fisher-Rao metric on the probability simplex and the Fubini-Study metric on pure states, specifically employing a square-root chart to transform the problem into a 1-Lipschitz self-map problem on a spherical orthant.
model_or_system: A finite-dimensional Hilbert space (dimension d >= 2) and the associated projective pure-state space (CP^{d-1}) with a fixed rank-1 PVM.
key_observables: Classical Fisher information, Quantum Fisher information, and the readout probabilities of the PVM.
important_parameters: The dimension d of the Hilbert space (result is dimension-wise).
main_assumptions: The result is restricted to fixed dimensions, pure states, and a single fixed rank-1 PVM; it assumes square-root regularity, the Cramér-Rao bound (Fisher non-expansion), and vertex calibration.
paper_structure: The paper develops a vertex rigidity lemma, establishes a theorem for Fisher-non-expanding maps on the probability simplex, and concludes with the primary theorem for PVM readout rigidity, followed by corollaries regarding escort-class Born uniqueness.
why_interesting: This work provides a fundamental, metric-based justification for the Born rule, which is central to the interpretation of quantum mechanics and the operational understanding of quantum measurements in quantum information.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
